persons.arguments_against: The strongest counter-case is a same-name relative: the corpus holds 5 Mainwarings and 2 biographies, so a father/son or brother could share the surname and even a colony. This fails because the match is not on surname alone but on a highly specific multi-dimensional fingerprint: all three initials (K.H.A.) agree, the distinctive naval rank 'Captain' is carried in every staff-list year and matches 'CAPTAIN K. H. A., R.N.' in the bio, and the exact unusual sequence of Jamaica judicial/harbour offices in the bio (stipendiary magistrate 1874, asst. pol. mag. 1876-8, harbour-master Kingston 1876, pol. mag. 1888-91) is reproduced position-for-position in the stint years 1877-1890. No relative would replicate three initials, rank, colony, and this precise office chain simultaneously.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second K. H. A. Mainwaring of Captain rank holding Jamaica magistracy posts in the same years would reverse this.

notes: Single biography, single stint; positions, colony, three initials, and rank all corroborate. High-confidence match.
